item.title: Mesalamine in Treatment of Intestinal Inflammatory Diseases-Formulation-Driven Strategies for Targeted Colonic Therapy and Remission Maintenance.
item.description: Drug development research - Review - Mesalamine (5‑aminosalicylic acid, mesalazine, or 5‑ASA) remains a cornerstone therapy for mild‑to‑moderate ulcerative colitis because of its local anti‑inflammatory and antioxidant actions on the ...

item.title: Ustekinumab in the Management of Perianal Fistulizing Crohn's Disease: Current Evidence and Practical Strategies.
item.description: Drugs in R&D - Review - Perianal fistulizing Crohn's disease (PFCD) is a severe and complex manifestation associated with high morbidity and limited treatment durability. Ustekinumab is a monoclonal antibody targeting the...

item.title: Comparative effectiveness of mirikizumab and risankizumab in patients with ulcerative colitis: A propensity-matched cohort study.
item.description: Indian journal of gastroenterology : official journal of the Indian Society of Gastroenterology - Mirikizumab and risankizumab are both anti-IL23 therapies approved for moderate-to-severe ulcerative colitis (UC); however, there is no comparative data to guide their clinical use. We performed an...

item.title: Combined Upadacitinib and Vedolizumab as 8-Week Induction Therapy for Moderate-to-Severe Ulcerative Colitis: A Multicenter, Randomized Controlled Trial.
item.description: Clinical gastroenterology and hepatology : the official clinical practice journal of the American Gastroenterological Association - Current advanced therapies for ulcerative colitis face an "efficacy ceiling" with endoscopic remission rates rarely exceeding 30%. We evaluated whether combination therapy-8-week induction with upa...

item.title: Vedolizumab for stricturing Crohn's disease of the terminal ileum - an international multicenter cohort study.
item.description: BMC gastroenterology - Crohn's disease (CD) frequently progresses to stricturing complications. Anti-stricture therapies are lacking, and anti-inflammatory therapies are hence the mainstay of medical treatment. However, ...

item.title: Acute severe ulcerative colitis: efficacy and safety of JAK inhibitors.
item.description: Expert opinion on pharmacotherapy - Review - Acute severe ulcerative colitis is a potentially life-threatening manifestation of ulcerative colitis requiring hospitalization and prompt intervention. Despite corticosteroids being the cornerston...

item.title: Comments on "Factors affecting 1-year persistence with vedolizumab for ulcerative colitis: a multicenter, retrospective real-world study".
Comments on "Efficacy and safety of mirikizumab in maintenance therapy for ulcerative colitis in difficultto- treat inflammatory bowel disease: a single-center retrospective study in Japan".
A comparative analysis of corticosteroids and exclusive enteral nutrition induction therapy in children with small bowel Crohn's disease: results of two prospective cohorts.
item.description: European journal of pediatrics - Multicenter study - Corticosteroids (CS) and exclusive enteral nutrition (EEN) are effective induction therapies for pediatric Crohn's disease (CD), but comparative studies evaluating long-term outcomes in small bowel...

item.title: Effects of guaiane sesquiterpenoids from the invasive species Ambrosia trifida on inflammatory bowel disease by targeting JAK2/STAT3 signaling.
item.description: Scientific reports - Ambrosia trifida, known worldwide as an invasive species, threatens agriculture and ecosystems and causes severe allergic reactions in humans. As part of the effort to valorize this harmful plant, ...

item.title: Efficacy and Safety of Risankizumab in Crohn's Disease: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of Randomized Trials and Real-World Evidence.
item.description: Digestive diseases and sciences - Crohn's disease (CD) management remains challenging, particularly in patients refractory to multiple biologics. Risankizumab (RZB), a selective IL-23 p19 inhibitor, has recently been approved for C...

item.title: Recurrent local and systemic hypersensitivity reactions after switching back from subcutaneous to intravenous vedolizumab in ulcerative colitis: a case presentation.
Repeated intravenous weight-based ustekinumab maintenance as an optimization strategy for Crohn's disease patients with loss of response to standard therapy.
item.description: Digestive and liver disease : official journal of the Italian Society of Gastroenterology and the Italian Association for the Study of the Liver - Some patients with Crohn's disease (CD) lose response to Ustekinumab (UST) standard therapy. Currently, there is limited evidence on efficacy of repeated intravenous (IV) weight-based UST maintenan...

item.title: Renal AA amyloidosis associated with inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: BMC nephrology - Renal AA amyloidosis is a rare but severe complication of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), associated with significant renal morbidity and poor prognosis. Data remain limited, particularly in unde...

item.title: Real-World Effectiveness of Upadacitinib for Perianal Crohn's Disease: A Multi-Center Retrospective Study.
item.description: Clinical gastroenterology and hepatology : the official clinical practice journal of the American Gastroenterological Association - A post-hoc analyses of phase 3 trials showed efficacy of upadacitinib (UPA) in perianal Crohn's disease (pCD), but real-world effectiveness is scarce and radiologic response has not been reported p...

item.title: From Bench to Bowel: A Review of Nanocarrier-based Drug Delivery Strategies in Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: Recent advances in inflammation & allergy drug discovery - This review aims to discuss various nanocarrier systems for treatment in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), encompassing ulcerative colitis (UC) and Crohn's disease...
